questions.question: Explain the difference between strong scaling and weak scaling with examples.
keyPoints: Strong scaling: fixed problem size, goal to reduce time
Weak scaling: problem grows with processors, goal to maintain time
Example of strong: same matrix multiplication with more GPUs
Example of weak: larger matrix multiplication with more GPUs

questions.question: A parallel program takes 100 seconds on 1 processor. With 8 processors, it takes 20 seconds. With 16 processors, it takes 15 seconds. Calculate the speedup and efficiency for both cases and analyze the scalability.
keyPoints: Analysis: Efficiency decreases with more processors
Possible reasons: communication overhead, load imbalance
Scalability is not ideal as efficiency drops significantly
